lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: Welcome, [brother/sister/my child].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Are we related?

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: We are all related, [brother/sister/my child], for we are all children of the flame.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okayyy...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what is this place?

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: You stand before the Doors of Dinh. Behind them lies the prison of the Wintertodt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a Wintertodt?

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: We do not know what it is. All we know is that it threatens to snuff out every flame in this land and plunge the world into eternal darkness.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear... We should probably do something about that.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: That is why we are here, [brother/sister/my child]. Ever since the Wintertodt was imprisoned, members of the Order of the Sacred Flame, or Pyromancers as we're often known, have stood vigil over this place.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: But alas, despite our best efforts, the Wintertodt is growing in power again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's just no good. Is there anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: Our order searches tirelessly for a way to defeat the Wintertodt. Until we discover it, the Wintertodt must be held at bay.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: However, I fear your abilities are not great enough to withstand the cold of the Wintertodt. Learn the ways of the sacred flame and then return to me.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: Pass through the Doors of Dinh to the prison within. You will find the finest of our order channeling the power of the sacred flame into the Wintertodt.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: Help them keep the holy fires lit so that they can keep the Wintertodt subdued.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I do that?

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: You will find the roots of the great Bruma tree have crept into the prison. Chop the roots and use them to keep the sacred flame alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: You can use the herbs of the Bruma tree to make Rejuvenation potions. Give these to any of our order who fall to the cold of the Wintertodt before they succumb to the darkness.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: How else may I be of assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Wintertodt.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: Do you feel the winds of winter as they lick across the land? This is the power of the Wintertodt. We know not what it is nor from where it came. Only that it wields the power of Winter itself.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: The ground on which we stand was once full of light, full of life. But then the Wintertodt came. Now the fires of these lands burn no more.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: How else may I be of assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about the Pyromancers.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: We are the Order of the Sacred Flame, formed over a thousand years ago when the Wintertodt first threatened Great Kourend.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: We fought against the Wintertodt until we managed to lure it behind Dinh's great Doors. Our task did not end there though, for we always knew that the Wintertodt would rise again.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: How else may I be of assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: I am Ignisia, Grand Master of the Order of the Sacred Flame. I have served our Order faithfully since I passed the Trials of Fire before the Ascent of Arceuus.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: How else may I be of assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do to help?

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: You are not currently strong enough to face the Wintertodt. Learn the ways of the sacred flame and then return here.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: We must hold back the Wintertodt until we can find a way to defeat it. Head into the prison and keep the holy fires lit so that our order may keep the evil at bay.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: The Wintertodt is no longer growing in strength but it still needs to be held back. Head into the prison and keep the holy fires lit so that our order may keep the evil at bay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I do that?

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: You will find the roots of the great Bruma tree have crept into the prison. Chop the roots and use them to keep the sacred flame alive.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: You can use the herbs of the Bruma tree to make Rejuvenation potions. Give these to any of our order who fall to the cold of the Wintertodt before they succumb to the darkness.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: How else may I be of assistance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fine thanks.

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: May the sacred flame guide you, [brother/sister/my child].

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: Welcome back, [brother/sister/my child]. How may I be of assistance?

sections.title: When attempting to plant Mithril seeds nearby

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: I get hayfever. Please take those flowers away.

sections.title: When attempting to set a trap nearby

lines.speaker: Ignisia
lines.text: I would not appreciate your traps near the bank. Please take them away.
